title: The Grand Narrative of Computing: A Timeline of Eras
intro: Computing history is not a straight line of progress; it is a series of philosophical responses to the "Software Crisis"—the recurring realization that our ambitions for complexity have outstripped our tools for managing it. Each era represents a new attempt to tame the machine, to make it more human-centric, or to harness its raw power at a scale previously unimaginable.

periods.era_or_period: V. The Web Explosion & Scripting Surge (1990–2005)
